objective.content_objective: Students will be able to explain how systems of law and banking enabled growth in trade, wealth, and peace
objective.student_goal: I will explain Roman systems using evidence and write on a graphic organizer (writing).
objective.wida_objective: Students will use language to explain how Roman law, banking, and Pax Romana enabled economic growth (ELD-SS.6-8.Explain.Reading/Writing) by using cognate awareness and graphic organizers with Portuguese-English vocabulary bridges appropriate for WIDA levels 2-5, producing a written explanation that demonstrates understanding of cause-effect relationships in historical systems.

anticipatory_set.original_content: Students will respond to an everybody writes.
anticipatory_set.bilingual_bridge: Preview key Portuguese-English cognates on board: lei/law, banco/bank, comércio/trade, paz/peace. Quick L1 connection: 'Como as leis ajudam o comércio?' (How do laws help trade?)

vocabulary_cognates.relevance_note: Core concept for understanding Roman legal systems
Essential for describing how Roman institutions worked
Key economic institution enabling trade growth
Central to understanding economic growth concepts
Fundamental concept for lesson on economic systems
Important outcome of effective legal and economic systems

tailored_instruction.original_content: Engage / Introduction: Briefly introduce the key systems that helped Rome grow: Roman law, banking, and the Pax Romana.

co_teaching_model.rationale: Mixed range (4+ levels) needs differentiated stations
co_teaching_model.wida_context: Mixed range with typical 7th grade distribution (Levels 2-5), requiring differentiated small-group support

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Introduce stations and preview vocabulary in L1
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Explain content objective and station expectations

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Station 1: Intensive vocabulary work with Levels 2-3
phase_plan.details: Station 3 (Independent): Bilingual materials practice. Rotation: 10 minutes per station

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Whole-group debrief and exit ticket check
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Whole-group debrief and exit ticket check

implementation_notes: Prepare 3 station signs, rotation chart, visual timer
Station 1 materials: Cognate cards, bilingual vocabulary chart

ell_support.implementation: Create bilingual vocabulary anchor chart with Portuguese-English cognates: lei/law, sistema/system, economia/economy, império/empire. Post visibly during slideshow.
Provide Cornell Notes template with pre-filled headers in both languages. Left column: 'Conceitos Principais/Key Concepts.' Right: 'Detalhes/Details.'
Exit ticket frames: 'Roman ___ helped Rome grow because ___.' / 'O sistema romano de ___ ajudou porque ___.'

special_needs_support: Visual timeline of Roman systems; color-code law (blue), banking (green), Pax Romana (gold)

misconceptions.original_content: Students may believe there is no correlation between trade and law

linguistic_note.note: Portuguese speakers often drop subject pronouns ('Go to school' instead of 'I go to school') because verb conjugation shows the person
linguistic_note.prevention_tip: Use sentence frames that explicitly include subject pronouns: 'I [verb]...', 'She [verb]...', 'They [verb]...'

supports_by_level.levels_1_2: Sentence frame provided, word bank with cognates, allow L1 draft then translate
supports_by_level.levels_3_4: Sentence starter provided, cognate chart reference, bilingual dictionary allowed
supports_by_level.levels_5_6: Open response, optional sentence frames, focus on academic vocabulary

bilingual_overlay.scoring_lens: Accept responses showing understanding of cause-effect relationship; credit use of academic vocabulary (law/lei, banking/banco, peace/paz); allow code-switching if meaning is clear
bilingual_overlay.constraints_honored: No new materials; uses existing exit ticket format

homework.family_connection: Ask family: 'Como as leis ajudam nossa comunidade?' (How do laws help our community?) Discuss in Portuguese/English and share one example in class.

objective.content_objective: Students will analyze Roman engineering achievements
objective.student_goal: I will describe Roman buildings using evidence and write notes (writing).
objective.wida_objective: Students will use language to describe Roman engineering (ELD-SS.6-8.Inform.Speaking/Writing) using visual aids and sentence frames appropriate for WIDA levels 2-5.

anticipatory_set.bilingual_bridge: Preview cognates: aqueduto/aqueduct, coliseu/coliseum, ponte/bridge. Ask: 'Que construções romanas você conhece?'

vocabulary_cognates.relevance_note: Core concept for understanding Roman legal systems
Essential for describing how Roman institutions worked
Key economic institution enabling trade growth
Central to understanding economic growth concepts
Fundamental concept for lesson on economic systems
Important outcome of effective legal and economic systems

co_teaching_model.rationale: Balanced proficiency distribution allows parallel groups
co_teaching_model.wida_context: Levels 2-5 with balanced distribution, both teachers can lead content instruction

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Group A: Content delivery with L1 support and visuals
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Group B: Content delivery with academic language focus

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Group A: Guided practice with bilingual materials
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Group B: Independent practice with scaffolds
phase_plan.details: Parallel practice; bilingual teacher supports Group A.

implementation_notes: Divide class into two balanced groups by proficiency
Both groups cover same content with different scaffolding

ell_support.implementation: Use images of Roman structures with bilingual labels
Highlight architecture cognates in both languages

special_needs_support: Provide 3D models or diagrams of Roman structures

misconceptions.original_content: Students may think all ancient civilizations had similar technology

linguistic_note.note: Portuguese places adjectives after nouns ('casa grande' = house big). English places adjectives before nouns.
linguistic_note.prevention_tip: Visual word order chart: Article + Adjective + Noun. Color-code adjectives (blue) before nouns (red).

bilingual_overlay.instrument: Label diagram of Roman aqueduct (5-7 labels)

supports_by_level.levels_1_2: Word bank provided in both languages, allow drawing with labels
supports_by_level.levels_3_4: Word bank in English only, sentence frames for descriptions
supports_by_level.levels_5_6: No word bank, write short descriptions of each part

bilingual_overlay.scoring_lens: Credit accurate identification of structures; accept Portuguese terms if concept is clear; focus on technical vocabulary acquisition
bilingual_overlay.constraints_honored: Uses existing diagram from textbook; no new materials

homework.family_connection: Find examples of arches or bridges in your community. Take a photo or draw a picture. Discuss: 'Por que os arcos são fortes?' (Why are arches strong?)

objective.content_objective: Students will compare Roman and modern government systems
objective.student_goal: I will compare governments using a Venn diagram and speak with a partner (speaking).
objective.wida_objective: Students will use language to compare government systems (ELD-SS.6-8.Argue.Writing) using graphic organizers and contrastive analysis appropriate for WIDA levels 3-5.

anticipatory_set.bilingual_bridge: Preview: governo/government, democracia/democracy, república/republic. Connect to Brazil's government structure.

vocabulary_cognates.relevance_note: Core concept for understanding Roman legal systems
Essential for describing how Roman institutions worked
Key economic institution enabling trade growth
Central to understanding economic growth concepts
Fundamental concept for lesson on economic systems
Important outcome of effective legal and economic systems

tailored_instruction.original_content: Compare Roman Republic to modern democracy.

co_teaching_model.rationale: Complex comparison benefits from dual perspectives and translanguaging
co_teaching_model.wida_context: Levels 3-5 dominant, can handle complex content with strategic L1 support

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Co-present with L1 clarification of key concepts
phase_plan.details: Both teachers alternate explaining, bilingual teacher bridges concepts in Portuguese
Students complete Venn in pairs; teachers circulate.

implementation_notes: Plan transitions and who speaks when
Bilingual teacher clarifies complex terms in L1 as needed

ell_support.implementation: Provide Venn diagram template with bilingual headers: 'República Romana/Roman Republic', 'Ambos/Both', 'Democracia Moderna/Modern Democracy'
Explicitly compare government terms in Portuguese and English, noting similarities and differences
Allow students to use both languages when discussing complex concepts, then translate to English for writing

special_needs_support: Provide partially completed Venn diagram with some examples filled in

misconceptions.original_content: Students may think democracy is a modern invention

linguistic_note.note: Portuguese 'atual' means 'current/present-day'. English 'actual' means 'real/factual'. Students may confuse these.
linguistic_note.prevention_tip: Explicit vocabulary: 'Actual = real (not current). Current in Portuguese is atual.' Use contrastive examples.

assessment.primary_assessment: Completed Venn diagram with 5+ items in each section

bilingual_overlay.instrument: Venn diagram comparing Roman and modern government (5+ items per section)

supports_by_level.levels_3_4: Sentence starters provided, word bank available, allow L1 notes then translate
supports_by_level.levels_5_6: Independent completion, focus on academic vocabulary and complete sentences

bilingual_overlay.scoring_lens: Credit accurate comparisons; accept code-switching in notes; require English for final submission; focus on conceptual understanding over grammar
bilingual_overlay.constraints_honored: Uses standard Venn diagram format; no additional materials

homework.original_content: Complete Venn diagram if not finished
homework.family_connection: Discuss with family: 'Como funciona o governo no Brasil? E nos Estados Unidos?' (How does government work in Brazil? And in the United States?) Share one similarity and one difference.

objective.content_objective: Students will evaluate the legacy of Roman civilization
objective.student_goal: I will explain Roman influence on today using examples and write a paragraph (writing).
objective.wida_objective: Students will use language to explain Roman legacy (ELD-SS.6-8.Explain.Speaking/Writing) using evidence and academic vocabulary appropriate for WIDA levels 3-5.

anticipatory_set.original_content: Quick write: What do we still use from Rome?
anticipatory_set.bilingual_bridge: Brainstorm in pairs (Portuguese OK): 'O que usamos hoje que vem dos romanos?' Share out in English with bilingual teacher support.

vocabulary_cognates.relevance_note: Core concept for understanding Roman legal systems
Essential for describing how Roman institutions worked
Key economic institution enabling trade growth
Central to understanding economic growth concepts
Fundamental concept for lesson on economic systems
Important outcome of effective legal and economic systems

tailored_instruction.original_content: Discuss Roman contributions to modern society: language, law, architecture, engineering.

co_teaching_model.rationale: Small group of Levels 3-4 needs intensive support for complex synthesis
co_teaching_model.wida_context: Most students Levels 4-5, but 5-6 students at Levels 3 need targeted instruction

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Small group (Levels 3): Pre-teach key vocabulary and concepts with L1 support
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Large group (Levels 4-5): Introduction to Roman legacy
phase_plan.details: Split then rejoin for practice and closure.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Small group rejoins; provide additional scaffolding during practice
phase_plan.details: Whole-group practice; small group gets extra support.

implementation_notes: Identify 5-6 students for small group ahead of time

ell_support.implementation: Pre-teach tier 3 vocabulary: legacy, influence, contribution, innovation. Provide definitions in both languages with examples.
Provide frames: 'The Romans contributed ___ to modern society. We still use this today because ___.  / 'Os romanos contribuíram ___ para a sociedade moderna. Ainda usamos isso hoje porque ___.'
Create cognate chart: legado/legacy, influência/influence, contribuição/contribution, inovação/innovation

special_needs_support: Provide graphic organizer with categories: Language, Law, Architecture, Engineering

misconceptions.original_content: Students may think ancient civilizations have no relevance today

linguistic_note.note: Portuguese rarely has consonant clusters at word end. Students may drop -ed endings: 'walk' instead of 'walked'
linguistic_note.prevention_tip: Editing checklist: 'Did I add -ed to past tense verbs?' Highlight all verbs and check for -ed endings.

assessment.primary_assessment: Written paragraph explaining one Roman contribution

bilingual_overlay.instrument: Written paragraph (5-7 sentences) explaining one Roman contribution to modern society

supports_by_level.levels_3_4: Sentence frames provided, word bank with cognates, allow L1 outline then translate to English
supports_by_level.levels_5_6: Independent writing, focus on academic vocabulary and complex sentences, optional sentence starters

bilingual_overlay.scoring_lens: Credit clear explanation of contribution and modern relevance; accept code-switching in drafts; require English final draft; focus on content and evidence over grammar; credit use of academic vocabulary (legacy, influence, contribution)
bilingual_overlay.constraints_honored: Standard paragraph format; no new materials beyond sentence frames

homework.original_content: Revise paragraph for grammar and spelling
homework.family_connection: Share your paragraph with family. Ask: 'Que outras coisas dos romanos ainda usamos?' (What other things from the Romans do we still use?) Add one family idea to your paragraph.

objective.content_objective: Students will synthesize learning about Roman civilization
objective.student_goal: I will review Roman systems, engineering, and government by reading and writing (reading, writing).
objective.wida_objective: Students will use language to synthesize understanding of Roman civilization (ELD-SS.6-8.Inform.Speaking/Writing) using multiple modalities appropriate for WIDA levels 2-5.

anticipatory_set.original_content: Gallery walk of student work from the week
anticipatory_set.bilingual_bridge: Students add sticky notes in Portuguese or English: 'O que aprendi/What I learned' and 'Perguntas/Questions'

vocabulary_cognates.relevance_note: Core concept for understanding Roman legal systems
Essential for describing how Roman institutions worked
Key economic institution enabling trade growth
Central to understanding economic growth concepts
Fundamental concept for lesson on economic systems
Important outcome of effective legal and economic systems

tailored_instruction.original_content: Review key concepts from Unit One through games and activities.

co_teaching_model.rationale: Review activities benefit from differentiated stations
co_teaching_model.wida_context: Mixed Levels 2-5, stations allow targeted review at different proficiency levels

phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Explain rotation schedule and assessment preview

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Station 1: Vocabulary review game with bilingual support (Levels 2-3)
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Station 2: Concept review and practice questions (Levels 4-5)
phase_plan.details: Station 3 (Independent): Review packet with answer key (all levels). Rotation: 10 minutes per station

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Preview next week's unit assessment in L1 and L2

implementation_notes: Station 1: Vocabulary matching game, cognate bingo, flashcards
Station 2: Practice questions similar to assessment format
Station 3: Self-paced review packet with bilingual glossary

ell_support.implementation: Vocabulary bingo with Portuguese-English cognates, matching games, Kahoot with bilingual questions
Provide comprehensive glossary of unit vocabulary in Portuguese and English for review
Pair higher proficiency students with lower proficiency for review activities

special_needs_support: Provide review guide with key concepts highlighted and simplified

misconceptions.original_content: Review and clarify any misconceptions from the week

linguistic_note.note: Portuguese speakers may omit subject pronouns ('Go to school') or place adjectives after nouns ('house big').
linguistic_note.prevention_tip: Use sentence frames with explicit subjects and adjectives before nouns. Example: 'I [verb]...' and 'The [adjective] [noun]...'

bilingual_overlay.instrument: Observation of participation in review stations and completion of review packet

supports_by_level.levels_1_2: Focus on vocabulary recognition and matching; allow pointing and gestures; bilingual support throughout
supports_by_level.levels_3_4: Expect verbal participation in L1 or L2; written responses with sentence frames; peer support encouraged
supports_by_level.levels_5_6: Independent completion of review materials; verbal explanations in English; help support lower proficiency peers

bilingual_overlay.scoring_lens: Credit engagement and effort; focus on growth and understanding over accuracy; note areas needing additional support for unit assessment
bilingual_overlay.constraints_honored: Formative assessment only; no new materials

homework.original_content: Study for Unit One assessment next week
homework.family_connection: Review unit vocabulary with family. Create flashcards together in Portuguese and English. Quiz each other: 'O que significa...?' (What does... mean?)
